raw_response:
Herpangina (Option A) is relatively common among children due to its viral etiology and seasonal prevalence.
Small pox (Option D) was eradicated globally by 1980 but historically affected humans extensively before vaccination campaigns began.
Cherubism (Option C) is extremely rare, occurring almost exclusively in childhood and characterized by benign jaw lesions.
Dental cysts (Option B) occur commonly in adults but less so in children compared to herpangina and small pox.
\boxed{C}
Clinical reasoning:
Among the given options, cherubism stands out as exceedingly rare, primarily manifesting in childhood with characteristic features such as bilateral enlargement of the jaws. The rarity and distinctive clinical appearance make it uncommon even amongst pediatric cases. Therefore, Option C (\boxed{C}) is correct.
